textPlain: That title is quite loaded. You could also write "US government shuts down after Republicans refuse to compromise with Democrats". Such disagreements always come down to both sides -- either side could unilaterally solve the disagreement by giving in.
How long do government shutdowns last? How often do they happen?https://www.nbcnews.com/data-graphics/longest-government-shu...
We can't swear in the new senator from Arizona who coincidentally has the tie breaking vote to force the release of the Epstein files.
The Guardian's just a couple degrees off of the party line: http://hud.gov/
It’s shocking to me how little control the democrats seem to have these days over then narrative. I don’t see them in the news giving their spin at all really
